current_state: The top-level tsdynamics namespace is a flat dump of 95 public names (len(ts.__all__)==95): 5 family base classes, 6 derived wrappers, ~52 analysis/data functions, ~18 result classes, 6 state-space-geometry symbols, and 8 submodules, with no grouping or workflow structure. The 149 built-in systems are correctly kept under ts.systems.* with a lazy __getattr__ for ts.Lorenz back-compat (src/tsdynamics/__init__.py:256-266) and that part is good. Everything else is poorly organised: (1) the analysis package is already cleanly sub-packaged internally (lyapunov, dimensions, chaos, recurrence, entropy, embedding, surrogate, orbits, fixedpoints, basins) but flattens all 78 names into one undifferentiated public namespace; (2) ts.transforms, ts.engine, ts.solvers are NOT reachable from the top level (they raise AttributeError because __init__.py:28 never imports them and they are absent from __all__ and __getattr__), so the entire 17-function signal/feature transform suite is invisible to ts.TAB; (3) the first positional argument of the analysis surface is named NINE different ways (sys, system, sys_or_traj, source, map_sys, data, observable, x, series) with no model-vs-data convention; (4) every system analysis is doubled as both a free function and a method, and the trajectory verb differs by family (ContinuousSystem.integrate() vs DiscreteMap.iterate()); (5) 0 of 35 result objects expose a .plot() hook despite the deferred-viz mandate; (6) inconsistent homes - WrappedSystem (a subclassable base) lives in derived not families, Trajectory has three homes, Box/Ball/Grid are duplicated in data and top-level. Perf: the only obvious Poincare entry points are slow - PoincareMap.trajectory(300) = 11.5s, poincare_section(steps=10000) = 323s (5.4 min), with a cryptic plane=(1,0.0) argument.

pain_points.title: transforms/engine/solvers submodules unreachable as ts.x
pain_points.evidence: Live: import tsdynamics as ts; hasattr(ts,'transforms') is False, ts.transforms raises AttributeError. Absent from the from-dot-import line at src/tsdynamics/__init__.py:28, from __all__ (138-253), and from __getattr__ (256-266). Yet import tsdynamics.transforms works and ts.transforms.__all__ has 17 names (power_spectral_density, detrend, butter_filter, extract_features, spectral_entropy).
pain_points.user_impact: A newcomer doing ts.TAB never sees the signal/feature transform layer - a whole headline capability is functionally hidden, and even experts get a confusing AttributeError for a documented module.

pain_points.title: First analysis argument named nine different ways
pain_points.evidence: inspect.signature over 24 analysis functions yields first-arg distribution {data:8, system:5, sys:3, x:3, sys_or_traj:1, source:1, map_sys:1, observable:1, series:1}. E.g. lyapunov_spectrum(sys), fixed_points(system), poincare_section(sys_or_traj), return_map(source), periodic_orbits(map_sys), recurrence_matrix(data), permutation_entropy(x), lyapunov_from_data(series), zero_one_test(observable).
pain_points.user_impact: Nothing is guessable. A user who learns lyapunov_spectrum(sys) cannot predict fixed_points wants system=, recurrence_matrix wants data=, permutation_entropy wants x=. Keyword calls fail unpredictably; the latent model-vs-data split is never surfaced.

pain_points.title: No .plot() or export hook on any of 35 result objects
pain_points.evidence: dir() over Trajectory, OrbitDiagram, DimensionResult, RQAResult, GALIResult, BasinsResult, ReturnMap, SurrogateTest: every one reports plot=False. There is no shared AnalysisResult base, no .plot accessor, no to_arrays()/to_dataframe() contract.
pain_points.user_impact: The north-star is a future multi-backend viz suite (matplotlib+Plotly+GPU+three.js) that must attach to these result objects. Today there is no stable per-result (x,y,label,scale) contract for a renderer to consume - the structures are not viz-ready, so the deferred-viz design has nothing to hang on.

pain_points.title: Analysis namespace flat despite clean internal subpackages
pain_points.evidence: ts.analysis.__all__ has 78 names in one flat list; dir(ts.analysis) also shows the real subpackages lyapunov, chaos, dimensions, recurrence, entropy, embedding, surrogate, orbits, fixedpoints, basins. The structure exists in code and is erased at the public boundary.
pain_points.user_impact: Tab-completing ts.analysis shows 78 undifferentiated functions instead of ~10 navigable categories. A user cannot find the dimension estimators or the recurrence tools as a group - the opposite of scipy's scipy.optimize discoverability.

pain_points.title: Doubled verb surface plus family-specific trajectory verb
pain_points.evidence: ts.lyapunov_spectrum(lor) and lor.lyapunov_spectrum() both exist and are documented. dir(Lorenz()) shows verb integrate; dir(Henon()) shows verb iterate; both also have trajectory. Two ways to call every analysis, two names for produce-a-trajectory.
pain_points.user_impact: Users do not know which form is canonical (function or method), and must remember integrate-for-flows / iterate-for-maps even though .trajectory() works for both. Violates the one-intent-one-verb consistency that makes scikit-learn learnable.

pain_points.title: Inconsistent symbol homes (WrappedSystem, Trajectory, geometry)
pain_points.evidence: WrappedSystem in ts.__all__=True, derived.__all__=True, families.__all__=False - a subclassable base living away from the other bases. Trajectory is-identical via ts.Trajectory, ts.data.Trajectory, ts.families.Trajectory (three homes). Box/Ball/Grid/sampler/grid_points/set_distance appear in both ts.__all__ and ts.data.__all__.
pain_points.user_impact: Docs and imports point at different official homes for the same object; a user defining a custom system looks in families for the base classes and does not find WrappedSystem there.

pain_points.title: Only Poincare entry points are slow and cryptically parameterised
pain_points.evidence: Reproduced on tsdynamics.systems.Rossler: PoincareMap(sys, plane=(1,0.0)).trajectory(300) = 11.549s; poincare_section(sys, plane=(1,0.0), steps=10000) = 323.195s. plane=(1,0.0) is an opaque (component-index, value) tuple.
pain_points.user_impact: Maintainer's explicit complaint (Poincare sections are too slow). The API offers no fast batch-section primitive and the cryptic plane tuple is the only way to specify the surface; bifurcation diagrams over a PoincareMap inherit this cost (minutes per sweep).

proposed_design: Reorganise around the user's mental model - define, integrate, analyze, visualize, export - mirrored in the import path, synthesizing three proven scientific-Python patterns. (1) scikit-learn consistency: one verb per intent (.trajectory() canonical for all families, .integrate/.iterate kept as flavoured aliases), one first-argument convention (model-consuming analyses take system, data-consuming take data, collapsing 9 spellings to 2), results-as-objects-with-public-attributes. (2) scipy curated-flat-top-level plus deep-discoverable-subpackages: shrink the top level from 95 to ~28 curated names (the 5 bases, 5 derived wrappers, Trajectory, the 5 headline analyses lyapunov_spectrum/bifurcation_diagram/poincare_section/basins/recurrence_matrix, and the submodules), while every other symbol stays reachable at a guessable qualified path like ts.analysis.dimensions.correlation_dimension. Un-flatten ts.analysis into its already-existing ten category subpackages so ts.analysis.TAB shows categories not 78 functions, keeping the flat re-exports as a convenience so from-tsdynamics.analysis-import-correlation_dimension still works. (3) xarray accessor pattern for the deferred-viz keystone: a shared AnalysisResult base carrying to_arrays()/to_dataframe() and a lazy .plot accessor (a thin namespace: traj.plot.phase(), od.plot(), dim.plot.scaling()), with a backend-agnostic PlotSpec dataclass (labels/title/scale incl semilog/loglog/ticks) each result fills in, so the future matplotlib/Plotly/GPU/three.js suite registers against ONE stable per-result contract. Fix the three concrete bugs: make ts.transforms/ts.engine/ts.solvers reachable (add to __init__ import plus __all__, flag engine/solvers internal), rehome WrappedSystem into families, pick a single documented home per symbol. Add a named, fast Poincare section entry point returning a viz-ready SectionResult with a readable plane=(component, value, direction) spec.

before_after.after: od = ts.bifurcation_diagram(system=pmap, param='c', values=vals); od.plot()  # accessor, clean viz-deferred error until backends land | traj.plot.phase(); traj.plot.timeseries() | x, y = dim.to_arrays().values()  # stable export contract

before_after.after: Lorenz().trajectory(final_time=100, dt=0.01)  # one verb, all families | Henon().trajectory(steps=5000)

before_after.why_better: Readable plane spec instead of an opaque index tuple, a single fast named entry point, and a viz-ready result - addressing both discoverability and the (separately-owned) perf complaint.

migration_notes: Most of the win is organisational, not destructive - actual breakage is small despite the break-all-you-must license. KEEP: the flat analysis re-exports (from-tsdynamics-import-correlation_dimension and from-tsdynamics.analysis-import-correlation_dimension both keep working) and the lazy ts.Lorenz system __getattr__. CHANGE: first-arg keyword renames (sys/x/series/observable/source/map_sys/sys_or_traj to system or data) break only callers that passed the analyse-target BY KEYWORD (rare - most pass positionally); ship the old names as deprecated keyword aliases for one cycle to soften this. ADD: transforms/engine/solvers to the top-level from-dot-import and __all__ (engine/solvers flagged internal in docstrings). REHOME: WrappedSystem from derived into families (re-export from derived so the wrappers narrative is intact). PROMOTE+ALIAS: basins_of_attraction to ts.basins, orbit_diagram to ts.bifurcation_diagram (old names retained). VERB: unify on .trajectory() as canonical, keep .integrate/.iterate aliases (zero breakage). DEMOTE from curated top level (still reachable, still flat-re-exported): the per-category entropy/dimension/embedding/surrogate functions. NEW (additive, no breakage): AnalysisResult base plus .plot accessor plus to_arrays()/to_dataframe() plus PlotSpec - existing result classes gain these via subclassing. Single-home each duplicated symbol (Box/Ball/Grid canonical in data; Trajectory canonical in data) while keeping top-level re-exports. Sequence: (1) reachability plus rehome fixes (pure additive), (2) arg-name plus verb unification with deprecation aliases, (3) result-object AnalysisResult/.plot scaffolding, (4) analysis sub-namespace surfacing plus curated __all__ shrink.
open_questions: poincare_section/return_map legitimately accept either a System or a Trajectory - standardize the param name to data and coerce a System to a trajectory-producer, or split into two clearly-named entry points (poincare_section for systems, section_from_data for arrays)?

Should the free-function form remain fully public and documented, or be demoted to a thin convenience layer with the METHOD (sys.lyapunov_spectrum()) as canonical - which of the doubled verb surfaces is the real API in docs?
Where does the AnalysisResult base plus PlotSpec live - extend tsdynamics.data, or introduce a new tsdynamics.results module? (affects whether .plot is a data concern or a results concern)
How aggressively to shrink the curated top-level __all__ - is ~28 the right target, or keep the ~52 analysis functions top-level and only ADD the sub-namespaces (less disruptive, less clean)?
Keep .integrate/.iterate as permanent family-flavoured aliases, or deprecate them entirely in favour of a single .trajectory()? (domain readability vs strict one-verb minimalism)
Should kaplan_yorke_dimension be re-homed from analysis.lyapunov into analysis.dimensions where it conceptually belongs, accepting that it is computed from a Lyapunov spectrum?
Is the ~6.9s cold import acceptable, or should the reorg also lazy-load heavy submodules (SymEngine/Rust) behind module-level __getattr__ to cut import time?

current_state: Defining a system is clean and worth preserving. The step/state/reinit/trajectory protocol is uniform. Obscurities: three names for producing a trajectory and maps lack integrate; only lyapunov_spectrum is a method while about 60 analyses are free-function-only and invisible from the object; the first positional arg has 15 names across 60 callables; derived wrappers nest inside-out and Poincare has two overlapping APIs. Evidence: families/continuous.py:169, derived/poincare.py:161, analysis/lyapunov/__init__.py. Full detail in planning/polish-design/system-derived-interface.md.
proposed_design: Keep the clean system-definition contract. Resolve method-vs-function as a deliberate dual surface: free functions stay canonical, and the object-side surface is a small set of cached topical accessors (xarray-style) rather than 60 flat methods: sys.lyap.spectrum(), sys.dims.correlation(), sys.recurrence.rqa(), sys.chaos.gali(k=2), plus first-class verbs sys.fixed_points(), sys.poincare(...), sys.tangent(k), sys.project(...), sys.ensemble(...), sys.stroboscope(). Accessors bind the system and delegate to the free functions, so tab on sys. shows grouped accessors and the internal analysis grouping becomes navigable. Plus: one verb system.run(...) dispatching on is_discrete (old names as aliases); fluent derived builders reading left-to-right (Rossler().poincare(section='y', at=0.0).run(steps=500)) with poincare_section collapsing into sys.poincare(...).run and stroboscope inferring its period; and a batched vectorised crossing detector plus a run_until block-stepping primitive to kill the per-dt FFI tax. The accessor layer and run-verb are additive and ship before any breaking rename.

current_state:
The analysis layer (src/tsdynamics/analysis/**) is a flat re-export surface over ~12 self-registering subpackages with 19 result classes, each authored stream-by-stream (A-LYAP, A-CHAOS, A-FP, A-ORBIT, A-DIM, A-EMBED, A-ENT, A-RQA, A-SURR, A-BASIN) with NO shared result base and NO shared calling convention. Each function is locally reasonable; the aggregate is the "obscure" the maintainer flagged. Reproduced against the shipped v3.0.0 wheel with .venv/bin/python:

- First-arg names the SAME "system" role six ways: sys / system / map_sys / observable / sys_or_traj / source; and the "data" role three ways: data / x / series. A user cannot predict the next function's first-arg name.
- lyapunov_spectrum top-level signature is literally `(sys, **kwargs)` — help() reveals nothing; real params differ per family (flow: final_time/dt/burn_in/...; map: steps/reortho_interval with NO transient param; dde: final_time/burn_in/...).
- The discard-transient concept has FOUR names: burn_in, transient, n_cut, and implicit-none; and `transient` carries three different units (steps in orbit_diagram, crossings in poincare_section, time in return_map).
- zero_one_test takes a bare series while its chaos/ siblings gali/expansion_entropy take a system — inconsistent within one subpackage.
- The scaling-curve family (curve + fit region + exponent±stderr) appears in >=5 result classes naming the abscissa x/times/dims, ordinate y/divergence/log_growth/values/afn_e1, fit fit_slice/fit_region/none — so a generic .plot.scaling() literally cannot find the curve.
- ZERO of 19 result objects have .summary()/.to_dict()/.to_frame()/.plot (hasattr-probed all). Only 2 of 10 spot-checked results carry .meta provenance (the A-ORBIT pair); 8 carry none, so a printed result can't say what produced it.
- Many returns are bare and context-stripped: lyapunov_spectrum->ndarray, max_lyapunov/kaplan_yorke->float, zero_one_test->float (sign-ambiguous), optimal_delay->int, permutation_entropy->float (m/base/normalized lost), embed->ndarray, fixed_points/periodic_orbits->bare list (no to_frame/plot).
- Issue #117 reproduced exactly: MetaStore.__repr__ (families/base.py:199-201) prints record COUNTS not values: `MetaStore({'system': 2, 'dt': 1})` while m['system']=='lorenz' — provenance looks empty in a notebook.
- PERFORMANCE: poincare_section(Rossler, 500 crossings) = 20566 ms (41 ms/crossing) vs integrating the whole 500-unit span once = 413 ms. Root cause: PoincareMap._advance_to_crossing (derived/poincare.py:161-181) loops sys.step(dt) — one FFI round-trip into the Rust engine per dt. Measured per-step FFI overhead = 77x vs a batched integrate. A dense-block + vectorized-crossing prototype runs in 140 ms (147x faster), same crossings to machine precision. This answers "Poincaré sections are too slow."
- find_attractors uses `region`, basins_of_attraction uses `grid` (and has no seed=), basin_fractions back to `region`; Grid(lo,hi,counts) is three parallel arrays not (lo,hi,n) triples. Component selection is fragmented: component= on embedding/entropy/surrogate, observable= on return_map, NO selector on correlation_dimension/rqa/gali (must pre-slice).
- Model citizen to generalize: DimensionResult (dimensions/_common.py:189) — frozen dataclass, __float__, clean repr, log-log curve, fit_slice, local_slopes, scaling_window.

pain_points.title: No shared result protocol: zero results expose summary/to_dict/to_frame/plot; provenance nearly absent
pain_points.evidence: hasattr-probed all 19 result classes: none has .summary/.to_dict/.to_frame/.plot. .meta present on only 2 of 10 spot-checked (OrbitDiagram, ReturnMap); DimensionResult/GALIResult/RQAResult/RecurrenceMatrix/SurrogateTest/EmbeddingDimension/ExpansionEntropyResult/AttractorSet carry no provenance.
pain_points.user_impact: No uniform way to summarize, tabulate, export, plot, or trace any result; a future multi-backend viz suite has no seam to attach to.

pain_points.title: The scaling-curve concept has five incompatible field schemes
pain_points.evidence: Reproduced field lists: DimensionResult(x,y,fit_slice,dimension,stderr); LyapunovFromData(times,divergence,fit_region,lyapunov); ExpansionEntropyResult(times,log_growth,fit_slice,entropy,stderr); GALIResult(times,values,decay_rate()); EmbeddingDimension(dims,afn_e1,dimension). Abscissa x/times/dims, ordinate y/divergence/log_growth/values, fit fit_slice/fit_region/none.
pain_points.user_impact: A generic .plot.scaling() cannot find the curve — the concrete, demonstrated reason the mandated viz suite cannot be added cleanly today.

pain_points.title: Issue #117: MetaStore.__repr__ prints record counts, not values
pain_points.evidence: families/base.py:199-201 builds {k: len(v)}. Reproduced: MetaStore after recording system=rossler, dt=0.01, system=lorenz reprs as MetaStore({'system': 2, 'dt': 1}) while m['system']=='lorenz'.
pain_points.user_impact: Provenance looks empty/meaningless in notebooks; users distrust that any metadata was captured.

pain_points.title: transient/component/region conventions fragmented across the layer
pain_points.evidence: transient has 4 names (burn_in, transient, n_cut, implicit) and 3 units (steps/crossings/time). component= on entropy/embedding/surrogate but observable= on return_map and NO selector on correlation_dimension/rqa/gali. find_attractors uses region, basins_of_attraction uses grid (no seed=), Grid(lo,hi,counts) is three parallel arrays.
pain_points.user_impact: Same idea spelled differently per call; the 'obscure basin interface' and the transient-unit traps come straight from this fragmentation.

pain_points.title: Bare scalar/array/list returns strip the context needed to interpret or reuse them
pain_points.evidence: Reproduced: zero_one_test->float -0.016 (no K in [0,1] context, sign-ambiguous), permutation_entropy->float 0.457 (m/base/normalized lost), optimal_delay->int 16, lyapunov_spectrum->bare ndarray, fixed_points->bare list[FixedPoint] (no .to_frame/.plot).
pain_points.user_impact: Users can't tell what a number means or tabulate/plot a collection without manual bookkeeping; results don't round-trip into reports.

proposed_design:
ONE calling convention + ONE result model + a Poincaré perf fix.

CALLING CONVENTION. Primary input is always the first positional argument, named by kind: `system` for any System (flow/map/derived), `data` for a measured series/point set (Trajectory or ndarray (N,) / (N,dim)), and a named kwarg for an upstream result (e.g. basins=). Kill sys/map_sys/observable/sys_or_traj/source and x/series. A single shared keyword vocabulary, one spelling and one unit each: ic (array), seed (int|None), transient (time for flows / steps for maps; never 'crossings' — poincare gets skip_crossings), final_time (flow horizon), n (iteration horizon, replacing steps/n_rescale/max_steps), dt, component (int|str|None, replacing observable and the pre-slice gap), region (Box/Ball/Grid, replacing grid). lyapunov_spectrum gets a real top-level signature: lyapunov_spectrum(system, *, k=None, horizon=None, transient=None, dt=None, ic=None, method=None, seed=None) -> LyapunovSpectrum (k replaces n_exp; horizon validated to the family's unit). zero_one_test realigned to take a system and integrate internally like its gali sibling, with a data= series overload. Add an ergonomic ts.data.region([(lo,hi,n),...]) constructor and add seed= to orbit_diagram/poincare_section/return_map/basins_of_attraction.

RESULT MODEL. A lightweight frozen-dataclass base analysis/_result.py:AnalysisResult mixed into every return, giving four guarantees: (1) a great __repr__ for free, built from a declared _repr_fields() so the 12 hand-rolled reprs collapse to ~3 lines each with uniform precision and a +/- glyph; (2) .summary() — a multi-line human readout with a domain interpretation line (statsmodels pattern); (3) tabular export — .to_dict() always (stdlib) plus .to_frame() via lazy pandas (soft dep tsdynamics[frame], clear install hint); (4) a .plot accessor — one attribute that is callable AND a namespace of typed methods (result.plot.scaling(), result.plot.curve()), backend-pluggable (xarray register_*_accessor pattern), shipped NOW as a raising VisualizationNotInstalled stub so the seam + method names + data contract exist for the future matplotlib/Plotly/three.js suite. Every result carries .meta (Provenance built from the existing SystemBase._provenance(): system, params, exact call kwargs, version). A registry-QA guard asserts every registered analysis returns an AnalysisResult with .meta. A shared ScalingResult(AnalysisResult) subclass exposes ONE canonical schema (estimate, stderr, abscissa, ordinate, fit_region, intercept, __float__, local_slopes, scaling_window) so .plot.scaling() is generic; DimensionResult/LyapunovFromData/ExpansionEntropyResult/the GALI+Cao/FNN curves become thin subclasses keeping their domain names as read-only @property aliases. Bare returns are wrapped while keeping dunder coercion: LyapunovSpectrum (__array__, .kaplan_yorke), ScalarResult for max_lyapunov/zero_one_test/estimate_period/optimal_delay (__float__/__int__ + the dropped context like K in [0,1] or m/base/normalized), Embedding (__array__), and FixedPointSet/OrbitSet collection results that stay iterable+indexable (for fp in fps, fps[0]) but add .to_frame/.plot/.summary and a .stable/.unstable partition.

POERFORMANCE. Replace the per-dt step() crossing loop with a dense-block kernel shared by poincare_section, return_map(kind='poincare'), PoincareMap and StroboscopicMap: integrate(final_time=block*dt, dt=dt) once per block (one FFI call), find sign changes of g(y)=normal.y-offset in vectorized NumPy (as _section_from_data already does), refine each bracket with the existing cubic-Hermite root find, continue from the block's last state. Validated 147x speedup, identical crossings.

Fix #117: MetaStore.__repr__ shows latest values with an (xN) annotation for overwritten keys.

migration_notes: First-arg: sys/map_sys/observable/sys_or_traj/source -> system; x/series -> data. Transient: burn_in, n_cut, positional steps-as-burn-in -> transient (flow=time/map=steps); poincare 'skip crossings' -> skip_crossings. Horizon: n_rescale/max_steps/steps-as-how-long -> n or final_time; lyapunov_spectrum n_exp -> k. Component: observable -> component; add component (default None=all dims) to correlation_dimension/rqa/gali/etc. Spatial: basins_of_attraction(system, grid) -> region; add seed= to orbit_diagram/poincare_section/return_map/basins_of_attraction; add ts.data.region([(lo,hi,n),...]). lyapunov_spectrum: documented top-level signature replaces (sys,**kwargs). Bare returns become result objects but KEEP dunder coercion (__array__/__float__/__int__) so np.asarray(spec), float(d), comparisons survive the break. fixed_points/periodic_orbits collections implement __iter__/__getitem__/__len__ so 'for fp in fps' and fps[0] are unchanged. ScalingResult renames fit_slice->fit_region, x/y->abscissa/ordinate, dimension/lyapunov/entropy->estimate, with domain names kept as read-only @property aliases through one deprecation cycle. The Poincaré dense-block kernel is internal — the output Trajectory of crossings is byte-identical, so it is a pure speedup, not a break. MetaStore.__repr__ fix is repr-only. Every result gains .meta from the existing SystemBase._provenance() mechanism. SEQUENCING: land analysis/_result.py + the fixed MetaStore repr + the shared dense-block crossing kernel first (each independent and shippable alone); migrate the 12 analysis subpackages one at a time (they self-register, so no cross-edits); add the registry-QA guard 'every registered analysis returns an AnalysisResult carrying .meta' last so it fails loudly on any un-migrated stream.

open_questions: pandas hard or soft dep for .to_frame()? Recommend soft (tsdynamics[frame], lazy import, clear install hint); .to_dict() stays stdlib.
transient unit rule: per-call follows the horizon (time for flows, steps for maps) vs one global unit? Recommend per-call to match how each family is actually parameterized.
Collection results (FixedPointSet/OrbitSet): full collections.abc.Sequence subclass vs a light list-wrapper with the three dunders + to_frame? Recommend the light wrapper.
Ship the .plot accessor raising-stub now? Recommend YES — the data contract IS the deliverable under the 'design for viz now' mandate.

lyapunov_spectrum horizon: one polymorphic horizon= validated against the family unit (top-level fn) vs explicit final_time=/n= at the per-family method layer? Recommend polymorphic at the function, explicit at the method.
zero_one_test: add the system overload (recommend YES, for convention parity with gali) while keeping the series overload?

Expose the dense-block crossing kernel as a public crossings(system, plane, ...) primitive that poincare_section/return_map/PoincareMap/StroboscopicMap all call? Recommend YES — one fast kernel, four consumers.

current_state: Every "slow culprit" the maintainer named — Poincaré sections, bifurcation diagrams, basins of flows — is the same root cause: the Python analysis layer drives a continuous system one dt at a time through System.step(), and ContinuousSystem.step() (src/tsdynamics/families/continuous.py:304-336) does a COMPLETE engine.run.integrate() round-trip per dt — re-resolving the solver, rebuilding the output grid, re-marshalling the whole IR tape into Rust (crates/tsdyn-core/src/lib.rs:275 OwnedTape::copy_in + tape.build per call), constructing a fresh solver, and building a provenance dict — just to advance one step. Measured: ContinuousSystem.step(0.01) is 44.8 us/step of which only ~8 us is Rust; cProfile of PoincareMap.trajectory(100) on Rossler shows 58,553 integrate() calls where tsdynamics._rust.integrate_dense is only 0.486s of 5.7s (under 10%) — the other >90% is Python orchestration (make_output_grid 0.566s, solvers.resolve 0.501s, np.all(isfinite) 0.399s, _provenance 0.341s), all per-dt. Reproduced wall times: PoincareMap.trajectory(500) Rossler = 13,785 ms; orbit_diagram over PoincareMap(Rossler), 20 values = 34,141 ms; basins_of_attraction on a 2D flow (Lissajous2D, 900 cells) = 30,713 ms vs the same FSM over a map (Tinkerbell, 2500 cells) = 832 ms (~35x faster with MORE cells). The contrast/gold standard: ensemble(Lorenz, 2000 ICs, ft=50) on the rayon-batched path = 279 ms. Crucially, a fully-built, fully-tested Rust event-detection + dense-output engine ALREADY EXISTS (crates/tsdyn-engine/src/event.rs: integrate_events/EventSpec/HermiteStep, O(h^4) crossing refinement, divergence guards, 23 passing tests including a 4th-order gate and a parity test vs the v2 Python Hermite refinement) but is COMPLETELY UNWIRED — crates/tsdyn-core/src/lib.rs:638-648 registers 9 pyfunctions and integrate_events is not one of them, and PoincareMap re-implements its logic in a per-dt Python loop. The Rust bridge (bridge.rs) has only batch entry points and no resumable solver-state handle, so every step rebuilds the tape and solver from scratch.

Stream E-EVENT (#77) built it and it was never connected.

pain_points.title: run.integrate recomputes loop-invariant setup on every single-step call
pain_points.evidence: cProfile: per-dt, solvers.resolve called 58,553 times (0.501s), make_output_grid 58,553 times (0.566s), _provenance 58,553 times (0.341s), np.all(isfinite) (0.399s) — none of which change across a step() loop. run.py:309/339/416/1025.
pain_points.user_impact: Even after the bigger fixes, ~35-45% of the residual per-step cost is re-resolving a constant solver name, rebuilding a 2-element grid, and building a provenance dict that internal stepping never needs.

pain_points.title: Map orbit_diagram / basins loop step() in Python instead of using the Rust ensemble
pain_points.evidence: Logistic map step() = 10.58 us/step; orbit_diagram(Logistic, 600 vals, n=120) = 2,792 ms — a Python loop over step() (orbit_diagram.py:221-225) while the engine already exposes iterate_map / iterate_ensemble_final (rayon) used by run.ensemble.
pain_points.user_impact: Map bifurcation diagrams and map basins are 5-20x slower than the engine can already deliver via batched iteration.

proposed_design: Three layered, additive, answer-preserving fixes in priority order. P1 (the big win): wire the existing Rust integrate_events through a new FFI pyfunction (integrate_events_dense) and a run.crossings() entry point; the section plane g(u,t)=normal·u-offset is a one-output tape built once via the existing lower_expressions seam (no new IR). PoincareMap.trajectory(n) then becomes ONE engine call that marches the whole attractor and refines all crossings in Rust at O(h^4), replacing ~58k Python integrate() calls. Keep the current Python linear-interp loop only as the DDE / no-RHS fallback. Bifurcation diagrams over PoincareMap inherit this automatically. P2: collapse the per-dt step() tax for genuinely incremental consumers (basins FSM, generic protocol, StroboscopicMap). P2.1 (ship now): batch-ahead buffering in ContinuousSystem.step() — when step(dt) is called repeatedly with the same dt, integrate a chunk (~256 steps) in one FFI call and hand them out from a buffer, amortizing solver/grid/provenance setup; pure Python, zero Rust change, answer-identical for fixed dt; invalidate the buffer on set_state/reinit/dt-change. P2.2 (durable): a resumable Rust OdeStepper handle (built tape + Box<dyn Solver> + SolverState, with advance(dt) and advance_to_event(g)) — exactly what event.rs already does internally — making basins-over-flows ~30x faster without buffering heuristics. P3: route map sweeps through the existing iterate_map/ensemble engine instead of Python step() loops, and hoist run.integrate's loop-invariants (resolve once at reinit and cache the kernel+jac decision on the problem; skip provenance for internal stepping; special-case the 2-element single-step grid). Optionally expose a general, scipy-shaped system.integrate(events=[...]) so the wired event engine also serves A-RQA/A-BASIN/arbitrary stopping conditions, not just PoincareMap.

before_after.scenario: Poincaré section of Rossler (the named slow culprit)
before_after.after:
# one engine call marches the whole attractor, refines crossings in Rust
times, states = engine.run.crossings(
    self.system, g=self._event_tape,      # one-output g(u,t)=normal*u-offset
    direction=self.direction, n=steps,
    method=..., rtol=..., atol=..., backend=self._backend,
)  # tsdyn_engine::event::integrate_events — O(h^4) Hermite, divergence-safe
return Trajectory(t=times, y=states, system=self.system, meta=meta)
before_after.why_better: ~100x faster (PoincareMap.trajectory(500): 13.8 s -> ~0.13 s, the cost of integrating the span once). Same numbers — the engine path is already validated to match the Python refinement to ~1e-11. Crossing logic lives once in Rust instead of duplicated in Python.

# continuous.py:304 — a full integrate() round-trip per dt (45 us)
def step(self, n_or_dt=None):
    prob = ODEProblem(tape=self._engine_problem.tape, ic=self._state_now, ...)
    traj = integrate(prob, final_time=self._t_now+dt, dt=dt, ...)
    return traj.y[-1]   # resolve + grid + FFI + provenance, all per dt

proposed_workstreams.title: Wire Rust integrate_events through FFI + run.crossings + PoincareMap.trajectory
proposed_workstreams.acceptance: PoincareMap.trajectory(500) on Rossler completes in the ~0.13 s range (>=50x faster than today's 13.8 s); crossing times/states match the current Python refinement to <1e-9; DDE / no-RHS systems fall back to the Python loop; divergence still raises RuntimeError. New integrate_events_dense pyfunction registered in tsdyn-core/src/lib.rs with a bridge wrapper; one-output g-tape built via lower_expressions.

proposed_workstreams.title: Batch-ahead buffer in ContinuousSystem.step() (kill per-dt integrate tax)
proposed_workstreams.acceptance: ContinuousSystem.step(dt) repeated with constant dt drops from ~45 us to <5 us amortized; basins_of_attraction over a 2D flow >=20x faster; buffer invalidated on set_state/reinit/dt change; results bit-identical to per-step integrate for fixed-step methods and within tolerance for adaptive. No Rust change.

proposed_workstreams.title: Route map orbit_diagram/basins through the Rust iterate engine
proposed_workstreams.acceptance: orbit_diagram over a DiscreteMap (e.g. Logistic 600x120) >=5x faster and numerically identical; basins over a map uses iterate_map/ensemble where applicable; carry_state semantics preserved.

proposed_workstreams.title: Hoist loop-invariants out of run.integrate's per-call path
proposed_workstreams.acceptance: solvers.resolve + jac decision resolved once at reinit and cached on the problem; internal stepping skips _provenance; single-step output grid special-cased; per-step residual cost reduced >=30% with identical results. Measured before/after on a step() microbench.

proposed_workstreams.title: Resumable Rust OdeStepper handle (durable replacement for buffering)
proposed_workstreams.acceptance: An opaque engine stepper (built tape + Solver + SolverState) exposes advance(dt) and advance_to_event(g); ContinuousSystem.step()/PoincareMap use it; basins-over-flows ~30x faster; GIL/lifetime-safe; results match the batch path. Supersedes PERF-2 once shipped.

proposed_workstreams.title: Optional general events= API (scipy-shaped) for analyses
proposed_workstreams.acceptance: system.integrate(events=[...]) exposes the wired event engine (multiple events, terminal, direction) for A-RQA/A-BASIN/arbitrary stopping; PoincareMap is one consumer; documented and tested against the Python fallback.

migration_notes: No answer changes anywhere. PERF-1 swaps the Python O(h^4) Hermite refinement for the Rust O(h^4) Hermite refinement that event.rs already proves agrees to ~1e-11 (it has a 4th-order-accuracy gate and an explicit parity test against the v2 PoincareMap._refine formula). Keep the existing Python linear-interp branch (poincare.py:132-135) as the fallback for DDEs and any system lacking _rhs_numeric. The streaming PoincareMap.step(1) protocol stays; only the bulk trajectory(n) path reroutes to the batched event call. PERF-2 buffering must invalidate on set_state/reinit/dt-change — hook the existing _state_now/_engine_problem invalidation points; set_state mid-stream (used by max_lyapunov) must drop the buffer. PERF-3 carry_state sweeps thread the previous final state as the next IC — the engine iterate_dense returns the full orbit so the last row is the carry state, semantics identical to today. The 'diverge loudly' contract is preserved end to end: integrate_events already returns IntegrateError::NonFinite/StepCollapsed; surface those as the same RuntimeError the Python paths raise now. PERF-4 must keep provenance on the final user-facing Trajectory (only internal step() loops skip it). PERF-5 supersedes PERF-2's buffer once it lands; remove the buffer then to avoid two code paths.
open_questions: P2 sequencing: ship the trivial Python step-buffer (PERF-2, ~30x) now and design the resumable Rust OdeStepper (PERF-5) as the durable replacement, or jump straight to the Rust handle (cleaner end state, larger FFI surface)?

Event-channel generality: expose a general scipy-shaped system.integrate(events=[...]) (multiple/terminal events — future-proof for A-RQA/A-BASIN/arbitrary stopping) or only the PoincareMap special case for now? The engine already supports multiple terminal events.
Default chunk size for PERF-2 buffering (256?) and whether to expose it — too large wastes work when a crossing is imminent, though PERF-1 sidesteps that for Poincaré specifically.

Basins over flows: detect recurrence as a terminal re-entry event on the engine, or keep the proven cell-FSM but feed it from a resumable stepper (PERF-5)? The latter is a smaller change and preserves the validated FSM logic.
Should StroboscopicMap also move to a single batched engine call (its crossings are at fixed times, so a strided iterate_dense is exact and trivial) — bundle it with PERF-3 or treat separately?

dimension: VIZ-READINESS CONTRACT — the serializable plot-spec seam so a future multi-backend visualization suite (matplotlib/Plotly/three.js/GPU/animations) is addable by construction, with no churn to result types. Design only; no backend ships.
current_state: There is NO plotting and no plot seam in the shipped library — but a substantial DE-FACTO plotting layer already lives, uncodified, in the docs tooling, and it proves every result type already carries the right plot data. `docs/_tooling/make_analysis_figures.py` is 814 lines of bespoke per-result-type matplotlib where each `fig_*` re-derives the plot kind, axes, labels, scales and limits by hand (e.g. `fig_dimensions` reads `res.x/res.y/res.fit_slice/res.intercept`; `fig_orbit_diagram` uses `od.flat()`; `fig_recurrence` uses `rm.toarray()`; `fig_chaos` bakes `semilogy` into the call site). `docs/_tooling/figures.py:159-240` literally dispatches plot kind on DIMENSIONALITY (dim>=3→3D line, ==2→2D phase, else time series; spacetime→imshow) — exactly xarray's `.plot()` dispatch, hand-rolled. `src/tsdynamics/viz/__init__.py` is an explicit stub that reserves a future `.plot()` hook (decision D6). Confirmed plotting-free core: `grep` for `def plot|_repr_html_|PlotSpec` and for `import matplotlib|plotly` in `src/tsdynamics/` both return nothing. The result-type zoo (verified live) — Trajectory, OrbitDiagram, DimensionResult, RecurrenceMatrix, RQAResult, GALIResult, BasinsResult, ReturnMap, LyapunovFromData, SurrogateTest — each already exposes the exact arrays a plot needs. The sharpest gap: `poincare_section()` returns a BARE `Trajectory(dim=3)` with intent buried in `meta["plane"]/["direction"]`, so a renderer cannot tell a section (2D scatter on the plane) from a flow (3D line) — `fig_poincare` works around this by re-integrating the flow AND re-running the map by hand. (Tangential: that 600-crossing section took a measured 19.2 s, underlining why a self-describing, serializable, cacheable result matters.) The registry/plugin pattern to mirror exists and is clean: `registry.py:263` generic `Registry`, populated for `analyses`/`transforms` at `registry.py:374-376`, with entry-point discovery via `plugins.register_entry_points` at `plugins.py:144`.
proposed_design: A three-part contract, ~250 lines of pure-Python dataclasses + one registry line, "good enough to extend easily." (1) Every result type and `Trajectory` grows ONE method `to_plot_spec() -> PlotSpec` plus `.plot(...)` sugar; it returns a declarative, JSON-serializable spec and NEVER imports a plotting library. (2) `PlotSpec` is the backend-agnostic IR, synthesized from Vega-Lite's data+encoding+mark grammar but numeric-array-native: it carries `layers` (each a `kind` + channel→ndarray `data` + neutral `style`), typed `Axis` per dimension (`label`, `scale` in {linear,log,symlog}, `limits`, `ticks`, `tickformat`), `title`, `ndim` (1/2/3), `aspect`, `annotations` (reference lines/text like the r1=3 onset), and `meta`. The SEMANTIC `kind` is a CLOSED `PlotKind` enum the contract owns up front — the 'huge pool of plot types': TIME_SERIES, PHASE_PORTRAIT_2D/3D, SPACETIME, BIFURCATION, ORBIT_DIAGRAM, COBWEB, RETURN_MAP, POINCARE_SECTION, BASINS_IMAGE, RECURRENCE_PLOT, POWER_SPECTRUM, SCALING_FIT, DIAGNOSTIC_CURVE, LINE_FAMILY, HISTOGRAM_NULL, TRAJECTORY/ENSEMBLE_ANIMATION. Tweaks (`relabel`, `rescale`, `limits`, `ticks`, `style`) MUTATE THE SPEC, not the renderer, so 'set xscale=log / relabel / retitle' is IDENTICAL across every backend. Animations are not a separate type — an animated spec is a PlotSpec whose layers carry a leading `frame` axis + `meta['animate']`; a non-animating backend renders the final frame. (3) Backends are renderers of the spec, registered through a NEW `registry.renderers = Registry('renderer')` line next to analyses/transforms, discovered via the SAME `register_entry_points` machinery (a `tsdynamics.renderers` entry-point group). `spec.render(backend='matplotlib'|'plotly'|'threejs')` looks the name up and calls it; the matplotlib renderer is the ONLY matplotlib code in the repo. `.plot()` attaches on `Trajectory` (the lingua franca) and each result via a tiny `Plottable` mixin (which also provides a gated `_repr_mimebundle_` for notebook inline display, a no-op when no backend is installed, so core stays plot-free). The docs tooling is rewritten to consume specs and doubles as the matplotlib backend's golden regression oracle. Purely additive — no field renames; the one behavioral change worth making now is giving Poincaré sections a result that carries section intent.

pain_points.title: Semantic plot intent is discarded — poincare_section returns a bare Trajectory
pain_points.evidence: `ts.poincare_section(ts.Rossler(), plane=(1,0.0))` → `Trajectory(n_steps=600, dim=3, t=[299, 3.81e+03])`; intent only in `section.meta` keys ['derived','plane','direction','dt','system','params']. A renderer cannot distinguish a 2D section-on-plane from a 3D flow line. `make_analysis_figures.py:fig_poincare` (lines 549-560) works around this by re-integrating the flow AND re-running the PoincareMap by hand.
pain_points.user_impact: A future `.plot()` on a section would draw the wrong thing (3D flow) or require reverse-engineering meta strings; every section/derived result has to be special-cased per backend.

pain_points.title: An 814-line de-facto plotting layer re-derives kind/axes/labels/scales by hand, per result, per figure
pain_points.evidence: `docs/_tooling/make_analysis_figures.py` is 814 lines; each `fig_*` re-derives the plot manually — `fig_dimensions` (180-226) reads `res.x,res.y,res.fit_slice,res.intercept` and hand-builds scatter+fit+annotation; `fig_chaos` (275-323) hard-codes `semilogy` (the recommended scale) at the call site; `fig_recurrence` builds the binary colormap by hand. The data is already on the result; only the description is missing.
pain_points.user_impact: Every new plot/backend re-implements this; recommended scales (loglog/semilog), labels and aspect ratios live at call sites instead of on the result, so they cannot be reused or kept consistent across matplotlib/Plotly/three.js.

pain_points.title: Plot-kind selection is hand-coded dimensionality branching inside the renderer
pain_points.evidence: `docs/_tooling/figures.py:159-177` `_render_ode` branches: dim>=3 → 3D line, dim==2 → 2D phase portrait, else time series; `kind=='spacetime'` → imshow. `_render_map` (221-240) does the parallel map dispatch. This is xarray's `.plot()` dimensionality dispatch, re-invented and trapped in the docs renderer.
pain_points.user_impact: The 'what kind of plot is this' decision is duplicated in every backend instead of being a property of the Trajectory/result, so backends drift and the live 3D hero / web export can't reuse it.

pain_points.title: No serializable plot representation — results can't be cached, exported, or sent to a web/three.js frontend
pain_points.evidence: No result type has `to_dict`/`to_plot_spec`/`_repr_mimebundle_` (grep returned nothing). The Poincaré section that took a measured 19.2 s to compute cannot be serialized to a plot description and re-rendered/tweaked/animated without recomputation.
pain_points.user_impact: Rules out the maintainer's stated goals — web/three.js export, GPU rendering, animations, and full tweakability — because there is no portable description to hand a non-Python renderer.

migration_notes: Purely additive to result types: each gets a 3-8 line `to_plot_spec()`; no public field renames, nothing breaks. Add `src/tsdynamics/viz/spec.py` (pure dataclasses PlotSpec/Layer/Axis/PlotKind + a Plottable mixin, NO plot deps) and one line `renderers = Registry('renderer')` in registry.py next to analyses/transforms, wired with the existing `register_entry_points(registry.renderers, 'tsdynamics.renderers')` from viz/__init__. Core stays plotting-free until a viz stream lands the matplotlib/Plotly renderers (the only files that import a plot library). ONE behavioral change worth making now: give `poincare_section`/`PoincareMap.trajectory` a result that carries section intent (thin `PoincareSection(Trajectory)` subclass or typed `meta['plot_kind']`) — the single place today's model loses renderer-needed information. Old→new mapping: `make_analysis_figures.py`/`figures.py` `fig_*` functions collapse to `result.to_plot_spec().render('matplotlib')` + brand styling, and their current hand-rolled output becomes the matplotlib backend's golden regression oracle (free test suite, no new fixtures). `registry.renderers` joins the registry meta-QA sweep in `tests/conftest.py` (mirroring analysis_entry/transform_entry), and a property test asserts every result's `to_plot_spec()` round-trips through `to_dict/from_dict`.

proposed_workstreams.acceptance: src/tsdynamics/viz/spec.py defines PlotSpec/Layer/Axis/Annotation/PlotKind/Plottable; relabel/rescale/limits/ticks/style mutate the spec and chain; to_dict/from_dict round-trips arrays↔lists; no matplotlib/plotly import anywhere; importing tsdynamics still pulls no plot library.

proposed_workstreams.title: to_plot_spec() on Trajectory + every result type; Poincaré section intent
proposed_workstreams.acceptance: Trajectory (auto dim-dispatch + kind override) and OrbitDiagram/DimensionResult/RecurrenceMatrix/RQAResult/GALIResult/BasinsResult/ReturnMap/LyapunovFromData/SurrogateTest each emit a correct PlotSpec mapping the verified-live fields to channels; poincare_section/PoincareMap carry POINCARE_SECTION intent; property test asserts every result round-trips its spec; no behavior change beyond the section-result.

open_questions: PlotSpec placement: src/tsdynamics/viz/spec.py (recommended — co-locates the plot concern, core stays plot-free, Trajectory imports it lazily) vs. tsdynamics/data next to Trajectory.

Styling vocabulary: how opinionated should backend-neutral style keys be? Recommend minimal (color/cmap/lw/alpha/marker) + a separate named-theme layer (docs indigo→teal brand) applied at render time, to avoid leaking matplotlib idioms into the IR.
Should .plot(xscale='log', title=...) accept inline tweak kwargs (easiest-possible call) in addition to to_plot_spec().rescale(...)? Recommend yes — inline kwargs forward to spec tweaks.
Animation channel shape: a leading `frame` axis on each data array (recommended — compact, GPU-friendly, trivially sliceable to a static final frame) vs. a list of per-frame Layers.

Multi-result composition (flow + its Poincaré section + fixed points on one axes, as fig_poincare/fig_fixed_points already overlay): a compose([...]) helper that concatenates layers and reconciles axes (recommended) vs. spec_a + spec_b operator.
Default backend resolution: TSDYNAMICS_PLOT_BACKEND env var with first-registered/matplotlib fallback (recommended) vs. always-explicit backend= argument.

dimension: Consistency, naming, errors, docs, typing & migration strategy (cross-cutting glue track)
current_state:
v3.0.0 is correct and deep but the cross-cutting polish is uneven because each A-* analysis stream was authored independently. Reproduced live (Python 3.14.2 venv, compiled engine):

NAMING — the same parameter concept is spelled up to 5 ways across analysis/__all__ (/tmp/kwarg_consistency.py): transient/burn-in = 3 spellings (transient/burn_in/n_cut); run-horizon = 5 (final_time/steps/n/n_rescale/max_steps); delay/lag = 4 (delay/tau/lag/max_lag); neighbours = 4 (k/k_max/min_neighbors/n_neighbors); embedding-dim = 2 (dimension/m); theiler = 2 (theiler/theiler_window). Only `seed` (17 fns) and `ic` (5 fns) are consistent — proof a single convention is achievable. The first positional arg has 9-15 spellings for "the thing you analyse". Verbs split per family: Lorenz().integrate vs Henon().iterate (neither has the other; .trajectory is on both but under-advertised). Plural/singular drift: fixed_points / periodic_orbits / periodic_orbit.

ERRORS — bimodal. ~7 source files carry excellent curated errors (method=/backend=/set_state/with_params/traj[...]: they name the bad value, list valid options, suggest a fix) but there are 250 raise ValueError + 19 raise TypeError total, so most are bare. Poor ones leak internals (ic wrong-dim -> 'cannot reshape array of size 2 into shape (3,)'; permutation_entropy(system) -> 'float() argument must be ... not Lorenz'; dt=0 -> bare ZeroDivisionError; rqa('hello')/rqa(NaN) -> the WRONG message 'pass exactly one of threshold='). Worst: SILENT footguns — final_time=-5 returns a 1-step garbage Trajectory, lyapunov_spectrum(nonsense=5) swallows the bad kwarg, correlation_dimension(3 points) returns dimension=0±0, and lor.sigmaa=99 (typo) is silently stored while with_params(sigmaa=99) correctly raises — a true inconsistency. No exception hierarchy: only 2 custom classes (EngineNotAvailableError, TapeCompileError), no TSDynamicsError base.

DOCS — 24/24 headline analyses have a docstring but 10/24 have no runnable Examples; lyapunov_spectrum has NO Parameters/Returns/Examples (signature is `(sys, **kwargs)`, a getattr-forward at analysis/lyapunov/__init__.py:57). 33 files contain >>> doctests but doctests run NOWHERE (no --doctest-modules in pyproject.toml:179). CONTRIBUTING.md:114 says 'Type errors are advisory for now'.

TYPING — pyproject sets `[tool.mypy] strict=true` but `.venv/bin/mypy src/tsdynamics` -> 727 errors in 72 files, exit 1 (284 no-untyped-def, 175 override from the map _step/_jacobian contract, 62 type-arg, 61 no-any-return, 56 attr-defined). Wired into nothing. Hides a real latent bug: TangentSystem.state() reads self._ode_lyap (2 reads, 0 assignments -> AttributeError if reached). Stale unused pywt mypy override.

RESULT CONTRACT — /tmp/result_contract.py over 9 result types: .meta on only 2/9 (OrbitDiagram, Trajectory); NONE has .summary/.to_dict/.to_frame/.to_arrays/.plot/_repr_html_; scaling-curve field names diverge (DimensionResult uses x/y/fit_slice/dimension/stderr; LyapunovFromData uses times/divergence/fit_region/lyapunov with no stderr).
proposed_design:
Five enforced cross-cutting standards that make the swarm's structural work coherent and keep it coherent:

(A) ONE NAMING VOCABULARY — a frozen glossary (docs/contributing/glossary.md) fixing the canonical kwarg for every recurring concept: system / data (first arg), ic, seed, transient (time for flows, steps for maps), final_time, n, dt, dimension, delay, theiler, n_neighbors. Homonyms distinguished (gali's `k` is an order, not a neighbour count, so it stays). Enforced by a registry-driven test that fails CI on any banned spelling.

(B) ERROR STANDARD — introduce a TSDynamicsError(Exception) base in tsdynamics.errors with a small tree (InvalidParameterError/InvalidInputError/ConvergenceError/BackendError), multiply-inheriting stdlib types so `except ValueError` still works. Generalise the 7 good curated messages into a law: name the offending value, list valid options or the rule, suggest the fix. Validate early; NEVER silently produce garbage (final_time<=0, dt<=0, too-short data, unknown kwargs, unknown attrs all raise). Wrap numpy/engine internals at the public boundary instead of leaking them.

(C) DOCSTRING+DOCTEST STANDARD — every public callable gets NumPy Parameters+Returns+a runnable Examples block; replace lyapunov_spectrum's **kwargs with an explicit documented signature; turn the 33 existing doctest files into a CI gate (pytest --doctest-modules on a curated fast subset + full nightly).

(D) MYPY GATE — drive 727->0 in passes (annotate the 284 missing defs; codify a typed Protocol or documented `# type: ignore[override]` for the 175 map overrides; fix the genuine attr-defined incl. _ode_lyap; drop the stale pywt override); add a typecheck CI job; put `mypy --strict` in CLAUDE.md pre-push; flip CONTRIBUTING from 'advisory' to 'required'.

(E) RESULT-CONTRACT TEST — once the sibling dossier's AnalysisResult base lands, a registry-driven meta-QA assertion that every registered analysis returns an object with .meta/.summary/.to_dict/.plot-seam and canonical scaling-curve fields. This stops the 28-hand-rolled-results pattern reappearing.

All gates are registry-driven (tests/test_polish_standards.py), proven viable by the existing tests/test_analysis_registry.py harness, so new systems/analyses are swept with zero test edits. A one-page Consistency Checklist in CONTRIBUTING every PR ticks.

before_after.scenario: Wrong-dimension initial condition gives a domain error, not a numpy reshape error
before_after.before:
>>> ts.systems.Lorenz().integrate(final_time=10, dt=0.01, ic=[1.0, 2.0])
ValueError: cannot reshape array of size 2 into shape (3,)
# origin: families/base.py:398 resolve_ic — no mention of 'initial condition'
before_after.after:
>>> Lorenz().run(final_time=10, dt=0.01, ic=[1.0, 2.0])
InvalidInputError: initial condition has 2 components but Lorenz has dim=3
(expected an array-like of length 3 for variables ('x','y','z'))
before_after.why_better: Names the concept ('initial condition'), the actual vs expected count, and the variable names. Matches the quality of the already-excellent method=/backend= errors. Subclasses ValueError so existing handlers still catch.

before_after.scenario: Silent garbage on invalid horizon becomes an early raise
before_after.before:
>>> t = ts.systems.Lorenz().integrate(final_time=-5, dt=0.01)
>>> t
Trajectory(n_steps=1, dim=3, t=[-5, -5])   # garbage, no warning
>>> ts.systems.Lorenz().integrate(final_time=10, dt=0.0)
ZeroDivisionError: division by zero          # bare, from utils/grids.py:37
before_after.after:
>>> Lorenz().run(final_time=-5, dt=0.01)
InvalidParameterError: final_time must be > 0, got -5
>>> Lorenz().run(final_time=10, dt=0.0)
InvalidParameterError: dt must be > 0, got 0.0
before_after.why_better: The 'never silently produce garbage' principle: a wrong answer that looks plausible is worse than an error. Caught early with a domain message instead of a 1-step garbage trajectory or a bare ZeroDivisionError leaking from a grid helper.

before_after.scenario: Typo'd parameter name is caught on attribute-set, consistent with with_params
before_after.before:
>>> l = ts.systems.Lorenz()
>>> l.sigmaa = 99      # typo for sigma
>>> l.sigma
10.0                   # silently stored as junk attr; sigma untouched
>>> l.with_params(sigmaa=99)
ValueError: Lorenz: unknown parameter(s) ['sigmaa']. Declared: [...]   # but THIS path validates!
before_after.after:
>>> l.sigmaa = 99
InvalidParameterError: Lorenz has no parameter 'sigmaa'. Declared: ['sigma','rho','beta'].
Did you mean 'sigma'? (Set a non-parameter attribute via object.__setattr__ if intended.)
before_after.why_better: Removes a true inconsistency: the SAME typo is caught by with_params() but silently swallowed by attribute set. Routes __setattr__ of unknown names through the existing param validator, and adds a 'did you mean' suggestion.

before_after.scenario: One naming vocabulary: the transient concept stops having three names
before_after.before:
ts.zero_one_test(series, n_cut=100)        # n_cut
ts.periodic_orbit(sys, burn_in=2.0)        # burn_in
ts.orbit_diagram(sys, 'r', vals, transient=500)   # transient
# three names for 'discard the transient', different units, no mnemonic
before_after.after:
ts.zero_one_test(data, transient=100)      # steps (data/map context)
ts.periodic_orbit(system, transient=2.0)   # time (flow context)
ts.orbit_diagram(system, 'r', vals, transient=500)
# ONE name everywhere; unit follows the call's horizon; CI fails on n_cut/burn_in
before_after.why_better: A user who learns one analysis can guess the next (the scikit-learn consistency lesson). Enforced by the naming-gate test so it cannot drift back. Same fix applies to delay/tau/lag/max_lag and the 5-way horizon spelling.

before_after.scenario: lyapunov_spectrum gets a real, documented signature instead of an opaque **kwargs sink
before_after.before:
>>> help(ts.lyapunov_spectrum)
lyapunov_spectrum(sys, **kwargs)        # analysis/lyapunov/__init__.py:57 — getattr-forward
# no Parameters/Returns/Examples; bad kwargs silently swallowed:
>>> ts.systems.Lorenz().lyapunov_spectrum(nonsense=5)
array([ 0.898, 0.0001, -14.566])        # the typo did nothing
before_after.after:
>>> help(ts.lyapunov_spectrum)
lyapunov_spectrum(system, *, n_exp=None, final_time=None, transient=None,
                  dt=None, ic=None, method=None, seed=None) -> LyapunovSpectrum
# full Parameters/Returns/runnable Examples; unknown kwargs raise
>>> Lorenz().lyapunov_spectrum(nonsense=5)
TypeError: lyapunov_spectrum() got an unexpected keyword argument 'nonsense'
before_after.why_better: The flagship analysis is currently the worst-documented and silently swallows typos. An explicit signature makes help() useful, makes mypy honest, and stops the swallowed-kwarg footgun.

before_after.scenario: Renames in v4 fail with an actionable moved-to error, not a cryptic AttributeError (migration strategy)
before_after.before:
# v4 silent clean break — a real user's v3 script dies with:
>>> ts.orbit_diagram(sys, 'r', vals)
AttributeError: module 'tsdynamics' has no attribute 'orbit_diagram'
>>> ts.lyapunov_spectrum(sys=mysys)
TypeError: lyapunov_spectrum() got an unexpected keyword argument 'sys'
before_after.after:
# v4.0 loud clean break via a thin _migration shim (deleted in v4.1):
>>> ts.orbit_diagram(sys, 'r', vals)
AttributeError: tsdynamics.orbit_diagram was renamed to tsdynamics.bifurcation_diagram
in v4.0 (renamed for discoverability). Update: `bifurcation_diagram`. (Removed in v4.1.)
>>> ts.lyapunov_spectrum(sys=mysys)
TypeError: 'sys' was renamed to 'system' in v4.0; pass system=...
before_after.why_better: The NumPy-2.0 model: every renamed symbol/kwarg raises a precise message pointing to the new name, plus a migration table and an optional codemod. Costs ~one module, converts every breakage into a one-line fix. A kinder break that is MORE in the polish spirit than a silent one, without freezing the redesign in deprecation amber.

migration_notes:
RECOMMENDATION: a one-release LOUD clean break (NumPy-2.0 model), not a silent break and not scikit-learn-style multi-cycle FutureWarnings.

Rename freely in v4.0 but ship a thin tsdynamics/_migration.py shim that raises a precise moved-to error for every renamed public symbol (module-level __getattr__ over a _MOVED = {old: (new, why)} table) and every renamed kwarg (an @aliased_kwargs decorator that maps old->new and raises a one-line TypeError). Delete the shim in v4.1.

Why this over the alternatives: (1) a silent clean break punishes the few real v3 users with cryptic AttributeError/TypeError and no path forward; (2) a moved-to error costs ~one module and turns every breakage into an immediate one-line fix — it is a KIND break, more in the polish spirit than a silent one; (3) full FutureWarning cycles (warn now/remove in v6) are overkill for a small audience and would freeze the redesign in compatibility amber, against the explicit license to break. v3's own ethos was clean-break, but v3 deleted INTERNAL backends; v4 breaks the USER-FACING surface, where good moved-to errors are worth their tiny cost.

Ship alongside: a docs/migration/v3-to-v4.md table (one row per renamed symbol/kwarg/verb, mirroring the four dossiers' migration maps) and a scripts/migrate_v3_to_v4.py codemod (libcst) for the mechanical renames (sys=->system=, x=/series=->data=, orbit_diagram->bifurcation_diagram, .integrate/.iterate->.run). The codemod doubles as a completeness test of the rename table.

TRUE clean break (no runtime shim) for changes a shim can't paper over: bare returns becoming result objects (the __array__/__float__ dunders keep np.asarray/float/comparisons working, so this is largely transparent), the transient unit normalisation, and the lyapunov_spectrum **kwargs->explicit-signature. These get migration-table rows + codemod coverage but no alias.

SEQUENCING to de-risk the whole swarm: (1) land additive layers first with zero breakage — AnalysisResult base + .plot stub, object accessors, TSDynamicsError hierarchy, doctest+mypy CI jobs wired but not yet blocking; (2) then the renames behind the loud _migration shim; (3) then the semantic changes (result wrapping, transient units); (4) cut v4.0 with the shim, v4.1 deletes it.

OLD->NEW mapping is owned in detail by the three sibling dossiers (analysis-calling-conventions, api-namespace-tree, system-derived-interface); this track provides the MECHANISM that delivers all of them safely.
open_questions: Migration mechanism: confirm the loud moved-to shim for one release (v4.0 raises, v4.1 deletes), vs a pure silent clean break (cheaper to build, harsher) vs full FutureWarning cycles (heavier)? Recommend the loud shim.
Ship a libcst codemod (scripts/migrate_v3_to_v4.py) for the mechanical renames? Recommend yes — strongest polish signal and a completeness test of the rename table.

TSDynamicsError granularity: a small tree (InvalidParameterError/InvalidInputError/ConvergenceError/BackendError) or just a base + stdlib-subclassing leaves (InvalidParameterError(ValueError)) so existing `except ValueError` keeps working? Recommend the latter via multiple inheritance.
Attribute-set validation: raise on an unknown system attribute (catches typos, matches with_params) or only warn (some users set scratch attrs)? Recommend raise.
Doctest gate scope: all 33 files in CI, or a curated fast 'examples that must run' subset in ci.yml + full set nightly (some examples compile-heavy)? Recommend curated fast + nightly full.

mypy override strategy for the 175 map _step/_jacobian override errors: a typed Protocol/ParamSpec for the map contract, or a single documented `# type: ignore[override]` convention? Protocol is cleaner but more work.

Should the canonical first-arg be `system`/`data` (two names by input kind) as the siblings propose, and does the naming-gate test enforce exactly that split? Confirm the homonym carve-outs (gali `k` = order, not neighbours).

pain_points.title: Same parameter concept spelled up to 5 ways across the analysis surface
pain_points.evidence: /tmp/kwarg_consistency.py over analysis/__all__: transient=3 spellings (transient/burn_in/n_cut), horizon=5 (final_time/steps/n/n_rescale/max_steps), delay=4 (delay/tau/lag/max_lag), neighbours=4 (k/k_max/min_neighbors/n_neighbors), embedding-dim=2 (dimension/m), theiler=2 (theiler/theiler_window). Only seed (17 fns) and ic (5 fns) are consistent.
pain_points.user_impact: A user who learns one analysis cannot guess the next — every signature must be re-read. This is the concrete, measurable form of the maintainer's 'obscure interface' complaint.

pain_points.title: Silent footguns return plausible garbage instead of raising
pain_points.evidence: Reproduced (/tmp/err_probe*.py, /tmp/bug_probe.py): integrate(final_time=-5)->Trajectory(n_steps=1,t=[-5,-5]); lyapunov_spectrum(nonsense=5) swallows the kwarg and returns a normal spectrum; correlation_dimension(3 points)->dimension=0±0; lor.sigmaa=99 silently stored while with_params(sigmaa=99) DOES raise.
pain_points.user_impact: Wrong answers that look right are worse than crashes — they corrupt downstream science silently. The sigmaa case is also a true inconsistency: the same typo is validated on one path and swallowed on another.

pain_points.title: Error messages are bimodal — ~7 files curated, the rest leak numpy/engine internals
pain_points.evidence: grep: only 7 files carry hint-style errors vs 250 raise ValueError + 19 raise TypeError. Good: method=/backend=/set_state name the value+options+fix. Bad: ic wrong-dim->'cannot reshape array of size 2 into shape (3,)'; permutation_entropy(system)->'float() argument must be ... not Lorenz'; dt=0->bare ZeroDivisionError; rqa('hello')/NaN->wrong message 'pass exactly one of threshold='. No TSDynamicsError base (only 2 custom exception classes).
pain_points.user_impact: Users hit cryptic tracebacks pointing into engine/run.py or numpy with no domain framing, and cannot `except tsdynamics.TSDynamicsError` to catch deliberate errors.

pain_points.title: mypy strict=true is configured but produces 727 errors and is gated nowhere
pain_points.evidence: .venv/bin/mypy src/tsdynamics -> Found 727 errors in 72 files, exit 1 (284 no-untyped-def, 175 override, 62 type-arg, 61 no-any-return, 56 attr-defined). No mypy in any workflow/pre-commit/Makefile/CLAUDE.md. CONTRIBUTING.md:114 'Type errors are advisory for now'. Hides a real latent bug: TangentSystem.state() reads self._ode_lyap (2 reads, 0 assignments).
pain_points.user_impact: The advertised type safety is fiction; type regressions land freely and at least one real AttributeError-waiting-to-happen is hidden in the noise. No editor/CI signal for contributors.

pain_points.title: lyapunov_spectrum is undocumented and opaque; 10/24 headline analyses lack runnable examples; doctests never run
pain_points.evidence: /tmp/doc_probe.py: lyapunov_spectrum has NO Parameters/Returns/Examples (signature `(sys, **kwargs)`, a getattr-forward at analysis/lyapunov/__init__.py:57). 10/24 headline analyses have no Examples. 33 source files have >>> blocks but pyproject.toml:179 addopts has no --doctest-modules, so none run in CI.
pain_points.user_impact: help(ts.lyapunov_spectrum) shows only **kwargs for the flagship analysis; example code in docstrings is unverified and can silently rot.

pain_points.title: Result objects have no shared contract: provenance and export are inconsistent
pain_points.evidence: /tmp/result_contract.py over 9 result types: .meta on only 2/9 (OrbitDiagram, Trajectory); none has .summary/.to_dict/.to_frame/.to_arrays/.plot/_repr_html_; scaling-curve field names diverge (DimensionResult: x/y/fit_slice/dimension/stderr vs LyapunovFromData: times/divergence/fit_region/lyapunov, no stderr).
pain_points.user_impact: Provenance is missing from most results; no uniform export; and a future generic .plot() cannot find 'the scaling curve' because every result names it differently — the structural blocker for the deferred viz suite.

pain_points.title: #117 MetaStore.__repr__ prints record counts, not values
pain_points.evidence: /tmp/bug_probe.py: repr(meta)->MetaStore({'lyapunov_spectrum': 1}) while meta['lyapunov_spectrum'] is the real array (families/base.py:199-201). Reporter on Python 3.14.2, TSDynamics 3.0.0.
pain_points.user_impact: A result's provenance looks empty/lost in a notebook even though the value is stored — a user-filed bug. One-line repr fix.

proposed_workstreams.title: Frozen naming glossary + CI naming-gate test
proposed_workstreams.acceptance: docs/contributing/glossary.md fixes the canonical kwarg for every recurring concept (system/data first-arg, ic, seed, transient, final_time, n, dt, dimension, delay, theiler, n_neighbors); tests/test_polish_standards.py fails CI if any public signature uses a banned spelling or a non-canonical first arg; all analysis/family signatures migrated to the glossary; homonyms (gali k) carved out and documented.

proposed_workstreams.title: TSDynamicsError hierarchy + value-naming error standard + kill silent footguns
proposed_workstreams.acceptance: tsdynamics.errors defines TSDynamicsError + leaves multiply-inheriting stdlib types (InvalidParameterError(ValueError), InvalidInputError(TypeError), ...); every deliberate raise subclasses it; final_time<=0/dt<=0/too-short-data/unknown-kwargs/unknown-attr all raise a value-naming message; numpy/engine internals wrapped at the public boundary; an error-message gate test asserts the headline wrong-inputs raise the right TSDynamicsError and that the silent-footgun table now raises.

proposed_workstreams.title: Docstring completeness + doctest CI gate
proposed_workstreams.acceptance: every public callable has NumPy Parameters+Returns+a runnable Examples; lyapunov_spectrum gets an explicit documented signature (no **kwargs); pytest --doctest-modules runs a curated fast subset in ci.yml + the full set nightly; a docstring gate test asserts the three sections exist on every registered analysis.

proposed_workstreams.title: Make mypy --strict honest and CI-gated (727->0)
proposed_workstreams.acceptance: mypy --strict src/tsdynamics exits 0 (annotate the 284 missing defs; typed Protocol or documented type:ignore[override] for the 175 map overrides; fix genuine attr-defined incl. _ode_lyap; drop stale pywt override); a typecheck CI job runs on PRs; CLAUDE.md pre-push block + CONTRIBUTING updated from 'advisory' to 'required'.

proposed_workstreams.title: Result-object contract test (provenance + export + plot seam)
proposed_workstreams.acceptance: extends tests/test_analysis_registry.py so every registered analysis returns an object carrying .meta/.summary()/.to_dict()/the .plot seam, and scaling-curve results expose canonical (x,y,fit_region,estimate,stderr); fails if a new analysis returns a bare value or a hand-rolled result without the contract.

proposed_workstreams.title: v3->v4 loud moved-to shim + migration docs + codemod
proposed_workstreams.acceptance: tsdynamics/_migration.py raises a precise moved-to AttributeError for every renamed symbol (module __getattr__ over _MOVED) and a one-line TypeError for every renamed kwarg (@aliased_kwargs); docs/migration/v3-to-v4.md table covers all renames; scripts/migrate_v3_to_v4.py (libcst) rewrites the mechanical renames; the shim is scheduled for deletion in v4.1; a test asserts every renamed old name raises with a message pointing to the new name.

proposed_workstreams.title: Hygiene PR: #117 repr, dead _ode_lyap branch, stale config
proposed_workstreams.acceptance: MetaStore.__repr__ shows latest values with (xN) annotation (+ repr test); the unreachable _ode_lyap branch in derived/tangent.py is removed; the unused pywt mypy override dropped; ruff still green.
